revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 鹽竈神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=東京都港区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]5-19-7 |地図 = |祭神 = [[シオツチノオジ|鹽土老翁]]、[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]][[経津主神|経津主命]] |社格 = |創建 = [[元禄]]8年（[[1695年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''鹽竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]]の[[神社]]。
== 概要 == [[1695年]]（[[元禄]]8年）、[[仙台藩]][[藩主]][[伊達綱村]]が、領地の[[鹽竈神社]]より[[分霊]]を勧請し、[[汐留]]にあった仙台藩[[江戸藩邸|江戸上屋敷]]内に創建した<ref name="史跡散歩">俵元昭 著『港区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド3)』[[学生社]]、1992年、29-30p</ref>。
[[1856年]]（[[安政]]3年）、現在地にあった仙台藩江戸中屋敷に移転するとともに、一般人の参拝を許可した。[[明治時代]]になり、仙台藩関係者が去った後も「安産の神」として崇敬を受けてきた<ref name="史跡散歩"></ref>。
[[境内]]は旧仙台藩江戸藩邸だったこともあり、旧藩主家の伊達家が所有していた。[[1930年]]（[[昭和]]5年）、地元の要望を聞き入れる形で当時の伊達家当主[[伊達興宗]][[伯爵]]は、地元[[町内会]]「愛宕下町会」に土地を寄付し、町会が所有・管理する東京唯一の公園「塩釜公園」として開園した。[[1972年]]（昭和47年）に港区に移管された<ref name="港区">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html 塩釜公園]港区芝地区総合支所まちづくり課土木担当</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[御成門駅]]より徒歩5分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *俵元昭 著『港区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド3)』[[学生社]]、1992年

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is referred to as '''Shiotsuchi no Kami''' (塩椎神), while in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he appears as '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' or '''Shiotsu no Oji''' (塩土老翁/塩筒老翁). Additionally, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. V..."
revision.text:
'''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is referred to as '''Shiotsuchi no Kami''' (塩椎神), while in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he appears as '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' or '''Shiotsu no Oji''' (塩土老翁/塩筒老翁). Additionally, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. Variations of his name include "塩土老翁神" and "塩土翁神," with the character for "塩" sometimes written in its archaic form "鹽." Pronunciations include "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami" or "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami."
== Overview == The name "Shiotsuchi" can be interpreted as "Master of the Tides" or "Way of the Tides," associating the deity with control over tidal currents and navigation. In {{ill|Kiki mythology|ja|記紀神話}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji plays a critical role, offering guidance and information to key figures. His portrayal as a coastal deity who imparts wisdom has parallels to the "Old Man of the Sea" motif found in {{ill|Greek mythology|ja|ギリシア神話}}, such as {{ill|Proteus|ja|プローテウス}}. Additionally, he is revered as a god of salt production.
The shrine tradition of {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in {{ill|Shiogama|ja|塩竈市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}, regards him as the deity who guided {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|フツヌシ}} during their pacification of the land. Shiotsuchi no Oji remained in the area to teach fishing and salt production to the people, making him a prominent deity in these domains.
== Legends == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}’s account of the {{ill|Descent of the Heavenly Grandson|ja|天孫降臨}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji is identified as {{ill|Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja|事勝国勝長狭神}}. He offers his land to {{ill|Ninigi|ja|ニニギ}} upon his arrival at Kasasa Cape. Another tradition identifies him as a son of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}}.
In the myth of {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|山幸彦と海幸彦}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji assists {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}, who had lost his brother's fishing hook. He provides a small boat (or tightly woven basket) and instructs Hoori to let the tides guide him to the palace of the sea deity {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}.
During the {{ill|Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武東征}}, the Nihon Shoki recounts that Shiotsuchi no Oji inspired Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to head east, stating, "There is good land to the east."
== Shrines == Shiotsuchi no Oji is enshrined at notable shrines, including: * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} (Shiogama City, Miyagi Prefecture) – The main shrine. * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|ja|鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Takamu Shrine|ja|高室神社}} (Kakegawa City, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Okazaki)|ja|六所神社 (岡崎市)}} (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)}} (Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Shioya Shrine|ja|塩屋神社}} (Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|海幸山幸}} * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|ニニギ}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}

{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | Image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | Image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | Image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | Image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}}

{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | Image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | Image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}}

{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | Image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | Image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | Image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

{{Shrine | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = [[File:Shiogama_jinja(Shinbashi,_Minato)_01.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695年}}) }}

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972年}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695年}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695th year }} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695th year }} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}}

== Overview == In {{ill|1695|ja|1695th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|ja|伊達綱村|12=draft}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=draft}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩|12=draft}}, enshrined a {{ill|divided spirit|ja|分霊|12=draft}} from the {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社|12=draft}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=draft}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|ja|汐留|12=draft}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|ja|1856年|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政|12=draft}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=draft}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|ja|1930th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=draft}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|neighborhood association|ja|町内会|12=draft}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|ja|御成門駅|12=draft}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview == In [[:en:1695|1695]] ([[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8), [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]], enshrined a [[:en:Bunrei|divided spirit]] from the [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Shrine]] in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in [[:en:Shiodome|Shiodome]]<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In [[:en:1856|1856]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|qq=|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from [[:en:Onarimon Station|Onarimon Station]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|qq=|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97376118]] Wikidata name: Shiogama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = File:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato) 01.jpg | Location = [[:en:Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} | Founded = [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) | location = {{ill|Minato|WD=Q190088}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.663228|N|139.754331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview == In [[:en:1695|1695]] ([[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8), [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]], enshrined a [[:en:Bunrei|divided spirit]] from the [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Shrine]] in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in [[:en:Shiodome|Shiodome]]<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In [[:en:1856|1856]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|qq=|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from [[:en:Onarimon Station|Onarimon Station]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97376118]] Wikidata name: Shiogama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = File:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato) 01.jpg | Location = [[:en:Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} | Founded = [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) | location = {{ill|Minato|WD=Q190088}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.663228|N|139.754331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview == In [[:en:1695|1695]] ([[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8), [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]], enshrined a [[:en:Bunrei|divided spirit]] from the [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Shrine]] in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in [[:en:Shiodome|Shiodome]]<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In [[:en:1856|1856]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from [[:en:Onarimon Station|Onarimon Station]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview == In [[:en:1695|1695]] ([[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8), [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]], enshrined a [[:en:Bunrei|divided spirit]] from the [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Shrine]] in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in [[:en:Shiodome|Shiodome]]<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In [[:en:1856|1856]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from [[:en:Onarimon Station|Onarimon Station]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview == In [[:en:1695|1695]] ([[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8), [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]], enshrined a [[:en:Bunrei|divided spirit]] from the [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Shrine]] in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in [[:en:Shiodome|Shiodome]]<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In [[:en:1856|1856]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97376118]] Wikidata name: Shiogama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = File:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato) 01.jpg | Location = [[:en:Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} | Founded = [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) | location = {{ill|Minato|WD=Q190088}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.663228|N|139.754331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview == In [[:en:1695|1695]] ([[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8), [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]], enshrined a [[:en:Bunrei|divided spirit]] from the [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Shrine]] in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in [[:en:Shiodome|Shiodome]]<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In [[:en:1856|1856]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from [[:en:Onarimon Station|Onarimon Station]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview == In [[:en:1695|1695]] ([[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8), [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]], enshrined a [[:en:Bunrei|divided spirit]] from the [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Shrine]] in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in [[:en:Shiodome|Shiodome]]<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In [[:en:1856|1856]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from [[:en:Onarimon Station|Onarimon Station]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from [[:en:Onarimon Station|Onarimon Station]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview == In [[:en:1695|1695]] ([[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8), [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]], enshrined a [[:en:Bunrei|divided spirit]] from the [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Shrine]] in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in [[:en:Shiodome|Shiodome]]<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In [[:en:1856|1856]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from [[:en:Onarimon Station|Onarimon Station]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97376118]] Wikidata name: Shiogama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = File:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato) 01.jpg | Location = [[:en:Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} | Founded = [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) | location = {{ill|Minato|WD=Q190088}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.663228|N|139.754331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview == In [[:en:1695|1695]] ([[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8), [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]], enshrined a [[:en:Bunrei|divided spirit]] from the [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Shrine]] in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in [[:en:Shiodome|Shiodome]]<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In [[:en:1856|1856]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from [[:en:Onarimon Station|Onarimon Station]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].

Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In [[:en:1930|1930]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local [[:en:Chōnaikai|neighborhood association]], "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." In [[:en:1972|1972]] (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695}} ({{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|en|Date Tsunamura|WD=Q5227557|lt=Date Tsunamura}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain}}, enshrined a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=divided spirit}} from the {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|en|Shiodome|WD=Q1185910|lt=Shiodome}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|en|1856|WD=Q7676|lt=1856}} ({{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|Chōnaikai|en|Chōnaikai|WD=Q14947926|lt=neighborhood association}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|en|Onarimon Station|WD=Q1039085|lt=Onarimon Station}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).
