<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97376118]] Wikidata name: Shiogama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = File:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato) 01.jpg | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|en|Minato, Tokyo|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695}}) | location = {{ill|Minato|WD=Q190088}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.663228|N|139.754331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695}} ({{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|en|Date Tsunamura|WD=Q5227557|lt=Date Tsunamura}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain}}, enshrined a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=divided spirit}} from the {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|en|Shiodome|WD=Q1185910|lt=Shiodome}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|en|1856|WD=Q7676|lt=1856}} ({{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|Chōnaikai|en|Chōnaikai|WD=Q14947926|lt=neighborhood association}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|en|Onarimon Station|WD=Q1039085|lt=Onarimon Station}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = File:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato) 01.jpg | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|en|Minato, Tokyo|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695}}) | location = {{ill|Minato|WD=Q190088}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.663228|N|139.754331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695}} ({{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|en|Date Tsunamura|WD=Q5227557|lt=Date Tsunamura}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain}}, enshrined a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=divided spirit}} from the {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|en|Shiodome|WD=Q1185910|lt=Shiodome}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|en|1856|WD=Q7676|lt=1856}} ({{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|Chōnaikai|en|Chōnaikai|WD=Q14947926|lt=neighborhood association}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|en|Onarimon Station|WD=Q1039085|lt=Onarimon Station}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}.

The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930|1=1930}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|Chōnaikai|en|Chōnaikai|WD=Q14947926|lt=neighborhood association|1=Chōnaikai}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = File:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato) 01.jpg | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|en|Minato, Tokyo|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|1=Futsunushi}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695|1=1695}}) | location = {{ill|Minato|WD=Q190088|1=Minato, Tokyo}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.663228|N|139.754331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}; {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695|1=1695}} ({{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|en|Date Tsunamura|WD=Q5227557|lt=Date Tsunamura|1=Date Tsunamura}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=Han system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain|1=Sendai Domain}}, enshrined a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=divided spirit|1=Bunrei}} from the {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|en|Shiodome|WD=Q1185910|lt=Shiodome|1=Shiodome}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|en|1856|WD=Q7676|lt=1856|1=1856}} ({{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930|1=1930}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|Chōnaikai|en|Chōnaikai|WD=Q14947926|lt=neighborhood association|1=Chōnaikai}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|en|Onarimon Station|WD=Q1039085|lt=Onarimon Station|1=Onarimon Station}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|en|Onarimon Station|WD=Q1039085|lt=Onarimon Station|1=Onarimon Station}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato)]][[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Shiogama Shrine | image = File:Shiogama jinja(Shinbashi, Minato) 01.jpg | Location = {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|en|Minato, Tokyo|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}} | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|1=Futsunushi}} | Founded = {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695|1=1695}}) | location = {{ill|Minato|WD=Q190088|1=Minato, Tokyo}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.663228|N|139.754331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}; {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (鹽竈神社, Shiogama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695|1=1695}} ({{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 8), {{ill|Date Tsunamura|en|Date Tsunamura|WD=Q5227557|lt=Date Tsunamura|1=Date Tsunamura}}, the {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=Han system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841985}} of {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain|1=Sendai Domain}}, enshrined a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=divided spirit|1=Bunrei}} from the {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} in his domain and established the shrine within the {{ill|Edo upper residence|ja|江戸上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo upper residence|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸藩邸|1=Edo Hantei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551112}} of the Sendai Domain in {{ill|Shiodome|en|Shiodome|WD=Q1185910|lt=Shiodome|1=Shiodome}}<ref name="Historical Walk">Motoaki Tawara, "Minato-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide 3)," Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 29-30</ref>.
In {{ill|1856|en|1856|WD=Q7676|lt=1856|1=1856}} ({{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 3), the shrine was relocated to the {{ill|Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja|仙台藩江戸中屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Domain Edo Middle Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, its current location, and opened for public worship. Even after the departure of the Sendai Domain officials in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine continued to be revered as a deity of safe childbirth<ref name="Historical Walk"></ref>.
The shrine grounds were formerly part of the Sendai Domain's Edo residence and were owned by the Date family. In {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930|1=1930}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 5), in response to local requests, the then-head of the Date family, {{ill|Date Okimune|ja|伊達興宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Date Okimune|1=Okimune Date|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380857}}, donated the land to the local {{ill|Chōnaikai|en|Chōnaikai|WD=Q14947926|lt=neighborhood association|1=Chōnaikai}}, "Atago-shita Neighborhood Association." The area was subsequently opened as "Shiogama Park," Tokyo’s only park managed by a neighborhood association. In {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}} (Shōwa 47), the park was transferred to the administration of Minato Ward<ref name="Minato-ku">[https://www.city.minato.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/koen/shiba/06.html Shiogama Park] Minato-ku Shiba Area Comprehensive Office, Urban Development Division</ref>.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Onarimon Station|en|Onarimon Station|WD=Q1039085|lt=Onarimon Station|1=Onarimon Station}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.66125,139.75173|goal=35.66321,139.75433|name=Route Guide}}).
